question_text:
312. Burst Balloons

You are given n balloons, indexed from 0 to n - 1. Each balloon is painted with a number on it represented by an array nums. You are asked to burst all the balloons.

If you burst the ith balloon, you will get nums[i - 1] * nums[i] * nums[i + 1] coins. If i - 1 or i + 1 goes out of bounds of the array, then treat it as if there is a balloon with a 1 painted on it.

Return the maximum coins you can collect by bursting the balloons wisely.

 

Example 1:

Input: nums = [3,1,5,8]
Output: 167
Explanation:
nums = [3,1,5,8] --> [3,5,8] --> [3,8] --> [8] --> []
coins =  3*1*5    +   3*5*8   +  1*3*8  + 1*8*1 = 167

Example 2:

Input: nums = [1,5]
Output: 10

 

Constraints:

  • n == nums.length
  • 1 <= n <= 300
  • 0 <= nums[i] <= 100
input_format: Space-separated values as per problem arguments.
output_format: A single line representing the result.
explanation: We use entry-based dynamic programming. `dp[left][right]` represents the maximum coins we can get by bursting all balloons between indices `left` and `right`. We assume the last balloon to burst in this range is at index `i`. Time complexity is O(N^3).
